zh.lifeSummary: 當Mechtilde af Holsten於大約1220年出生在霍爾斯坦時，她的父親Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein11218歲，母親Heilwig zur Lippe23歲。她於1261年與Birger Magnusson Folkungaätten結婚。她去世於1288年在德國史萊斯維-爾斯坦吉爾享年69歲，埋葬在瑞典維亞斯特拉 格塔藍德瓦恩姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mechtilde af Holsten于大约1220年出生在霍爾斯坦时，她的父亲Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein11218岁，母亲Heilwig zur Lippe23岁。她于1261年与Birger Magnusson Folkungaätten结婚。她去世于1288年在德国史萊斯維-爾斯坦吉爾享年69岁，埋葬在瑞典維亞斯特拉 格塔藍德瓦恩姆。

nameMeanings.name: af
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German: from a shortened form of   Adolf  .
Swedish: from the personal name Alf, a short form of any of several personal names formed with alf ‘elf’ as the first element.

variants.name: Adolf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Gottlieb, Horst, Reinhold, Rinehart, Waltraud.

German and Dutch: from the ancient Germanic personal name Athaulf, composed of the Old High German elements ad (short form of adal ‘noble’) + (w)ulf ‘wolf’. The surname of German origin is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Czechia.

variants.name: Alfson
meanings: Americanized form of Norwegian Alfsen: patronymic from the personal name Alf .
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Alfsson, Swedish cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Holsten
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German: variant of   Holstein   or   Holst  .

variants.name: Holston
meanings: English (Middlesex and Sussex): possibly a variant of Halston, which is partly a habitational name from Halston (Shropshire), perhaps named with an Old English personal name Ealh + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, and partly derived from the Middle English personal name Halstein. Alternatively, Holston may be a habitational name from Holstone in Durham (from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + stān ‘stone’) or from a lost or unidentified place so called.
Probably an Americanized form of German   Holsten  .

variants.name: Holstein
meanings: German: habitational name from the province of Holstein, long disputed between Germany and Denmark. This gets its name from holsten, the dative plural, originally used after a preposition, of holst, from Middle Low German holt-sate ‘dweller in the woods’ (from Middle Low German holt ‘wood’ + sate, sete ‘tenant’). The final syllable has been erroneously altered by analogy with North German Steen ‘stone’, which in South German and standard German has the form Stein.

nameMeanings.name: Mechtilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.description: King Eriksson died. Valdemar crowned King. Birger Jarl declared regent until Valdemar is of age.

historicEvents.title: Earliest Mention of Stockholm
historicEvents.description: The earliest known recorded history of the city of Stockholm is in Norse sagas as Agnafit which dates to 1252. The city rose to prominence due to Baltic trade. Stockholm is now the most populous urban area in the region.

en.lifeSummary: When Mechtilde af Holsten was born about 1220, in Holstein, her father, Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein, was 11218 and her mother, Heilwig zur Lippe, was 23. She married Birger Magnusson Folkungaätten in 1261. She died in 1288, in Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Germany, at the age of 69, and was buried in Varnhem, Västra Götaland, Sweden.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mechtilde af Holsten est née environ 1220, à Holstein, son père, Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein, avait 11218 ans et sa mère, Heilwig zur Lippe, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Birger Magnusson Folkungaätten en 1261. Elle est décédée en 1288, à Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Allemagne, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Varnhem, Västra Götaland, Suède.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mechtilde af Holsten um 1220 in Holstein geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein 11218 und ihre Mutter Heilwig zur Lippe 23. Sie hat 1261 Birger Magnusson Folkungaätten geheiratet. Sie starb 1288 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Deutschland und wurde in Varnhem, Västra Götaland, Schweden begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mechtilde af Holsten è nata il circa 1220 nel Holstein, sua padre, Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein, aveva 11218 anni e sua madre, Heilwig zur Lippe, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Birger Magnusson Folkungaätten nel 1261. Morì nel 1288, nel Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Germania, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolta a Varnhem, Västra Götaland, Svezia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mechtilde af Holstenが約1220年にホールステインで生まれた時，父Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holsteinは11218歳，母Heilwig zur Lippeは23歳であった。1261年にBirger Magnusson Folkungaättenと結婚した。彼女は1288年にドイツスチレスウィグ-hオルステインキエルで69歳で死去し，スウェーデンvaストラ グoタランドバーンヘムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mechtilde af Holsten(이)가 대략 1220년 홀스타인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein(은)는 11218세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Heilwig zur Lippe(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1261년 Birger Magnusson Folkungaätten(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1288년 독일 슐레스위그-홀스타인 킬에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 스웨덴 브a스트라 그o탤란드 바른헤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mechtilde af Holsten nasceu aproximadamente 1220, em Holstein, seu pai, Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein, tinha 11218 anos e sua mãe, Heilwig zur Lippe, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Birger Magnusson Folkungaätten em 1261. Ela faleceu em 1288, em Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemanha, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Varnhem, Västra Götaland, Suécia.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mechtilde af Holsten родилась около 1220 г. в местности холстин, её отцу по имени Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein было 11218 лет, а матери, которую звали Heilwig zur Lippe, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Birger Magnusson Folkungaätten в 1261 г. Она умерла в 1288 г. в местности киел, щлесвиг-холстин, Германия в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности варнем, вaстра гoталанд, Швеция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mechtilde af Holsten nació aproximadamente 1220, en Holstein, su papá, Count Adolf IV. von Schauenburg und Holstein, tenía 11218 años y su mamá, Heilwig zur Lippe, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Birger Magnusson Folkungaätten en 1261. Murió en 1288, en Kiel, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemania, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en Varnhem, Västra Götaland, Suecia.
